key_highlights: Markets experienced a broad selloff with stocks posting worst day since March, driven by rising bond yields and inflation fears.
Kevin Warsh confirmed as new Fed chair, signaling potential hawkish shift; bond vigilantes are back as global bond rout deepens.
Cerebras IPO surged 90% on debut, marking the largest IPO of 2026 and highlighting AI chip demand despite broader tech weakness.
Indian rupee hit all-time low past 96 per dollar amid oil price surge, adding to emerging market stress.
Berkshire Hathaway amassed $2.6B stake in Delta Airlines while selling $8B of Chevron shares, signaling sector rotation.
Stagflation fears rise with nearly 40% probability by end of 2026, as oil prices and inflation pressures persist.
SpaceX plans IPO filing as soon as Wednesday, potentially reshaping space and defense investment landscape.
Tech job market remains under pressure with Meta reportedly planning 8,000 layoffs and software engineering roles declining.

sectors.trend: Mixed but under pressure from rising yields and valuation concerns; AI-related names like Nvidia and Cerebras show strength, but broader selloff hit chipmakers hard.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure and data center demand remain strong, but rising rates and geopolitical uncertainty could compress multiples. Focus on companies with real earnings and AI exposure.

sectors.trend: Oil prices volatile; crude slipped on dollar strength but rose on Trump-China tensions and Hormuz concerns. Permian gas glut forces producers to pay buyers. Berkshire sold Chevron shares.
sectors.implications: Energy sector faces headwinds from potential demand destruction and regulatory changes, but supply constraints and geopolitical risks provide support. Canadian pipeline proposals add long-term optionality.

sectors.trend: Banks and financials benefit from rising yields but face credit risk from emerging market exposure and potential stagflation. Forbright and Erock file for IPOs, indicating sector activity.
sectors.implications: Higher rates improve net interest margins but increase default risk. Focus on well-capitalized banks with diversified revenue streams.

sectors.trend: Severe stress: Indian rupee at all-time low, Turkey's Kontrolmatik defaults, emerging markets cap worst week since March on inflation fears. Dollar strength exacerbates outflows.
sectors.implications: Avoid broad EM exposure; selective opportunities in commodity exporters or countries with strong reform momentum (e.g., Argentina's YPF project).

sectors.trend: CVS highlighted as cheap long-term buy; BioCardia progresses with Japan filing. Sector relatively defensive amid market turmoil.
sectors.implications: Healthcare offers stability and innovation; focus on companies with strong pipelines and pricing power.

sectors.trend: Data center construction boom ($15B Wisconsin campus) and pipeline proposals support long-term demand. AECOM and Northrop Grumman remain steady.
sectors.implications: Infrastructure spending and AI data center buildout provide multi-year tailwinds for select industrials and engineering firms.

sectors.trend: Affordability crunch heading into summer; subscription fatigue rising; Ford falls after AI-driven rally abates. Consumers shifting to buses due to fuel costs.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary names face headwinds from inflation and weakening sentiment. Focus on essentials and value-oriented offerings.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates little changed despite inflation data; Zillow CEO preparing for next real estate revolution. Homeowners insurance and renters insurance questions indicate consumer focus on costs.
sectors.implications: Housing market remains resilient but affordability constraints limit upside. Technology-driven real estate platforms may gain share.

key_themes: Rising bond yields and global bond rout driving equity selloff
Stagflation fears with nearly 40% probability by end of 2026
AI and data center investment boom despite broader tech weakness
Dollar strength pressuring emerging markets and commodities
Geopolitical uncertainty from Trump-China trade deal lack of progress and Iran tensions
IPO activity remains robust (SpaceX, Cerebras, Forbright, Erock) indicating private market confidence
Labor market shifts: tech layoffs continue, AI replacing some roles, but data centers create few jobs
Commodity price volatility: oil, copper, gold all under pressure from inflation fears and dollar strength
Fed transition to Kevin Warsh signals potential hawkish policy shift
Consumer affordability crunch and subscription fatigue

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Rising bond yields and global bond rout
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in fixed income portfolios; favor floating rate instruments and short-term bonds. Increase cash allocation.
Allocate to inflation-protected securities (TIPS), commodities, and real assets. Avoid cyclical stocks with high debt.
Increase exposure to defense stocks (Northrop Grumman), energy (oil producers), and gold. Hedge currency risk.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Emerging market currency and debt crisis
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce EM equity and bond exposure; focus on hard currency debt of stronger EM countries. Avoid currencies like rupee and lira.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector valuation correction and layoffs
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on profitable tech companies with strong AI exposure and reasonable valuations. Avoid overvalued names with weak fundamentals.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price volatility and supply disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain energy overweight but take profits on sharp rallies. Use options to hedge against price spikes.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown and affordability crunch
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor consumer staples and discount retailers over discretionary. Monitor credit card delinquency data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed policy shift under new chair Kevin Warsh
risk_assessment.mitigation: Prepare for potential rate hikes; reduce leverage in portfolios. Favor banks and financials that benefit from higher rates.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and semiconductor leaders on weakness
opportunities.rationale: Cerebras IPO success and Nvidia strength indicate sustained AI demand. Wells Fargo raised Broadcom price target. Pullbacks in chip stocks due to macro fears are buying opportunities.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to defense and aerospace
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical tensions and new pipeline proposals support defense spending. Northrop Grumman and AECOM offer stable growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy infrastructure and midstream
opportunities.rationale: Permian gas glut creates opportunities for midstream companies that can transport gas. Enbridge and Enterprise Products Partners offer attractive yields and long-term contracts.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Berkshire Hathaway as a defensive play
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's recent moves (Delta stake, Chevron sale) show active portfolio management. Its cash pile and diversified holdings provide downside protection.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy select healthcare names for stability
opportunities.rationale: CVS is cheap with long-term potential. BioCardia has a clear regulatory path. Healthcare is defensive in stagflation scenarios.

opportunities.recommendation: Participate in high-quality IPOs (SpaceX, Forbright, Erock)
opportunities.rationale: Cerebras IPO success shows strong demand for innovative companies. SpaceX and data center-related IPOs offer exposure to secular growth themes.

defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields make cash attractive. Short-term bonds reduce duration risk while providing income. Wait for better entry points in equities.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against dollar strength and EM currency risk
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar is at its best week since March. EM currencies like rupee are at all-time lows. Use currency hedges or USD-denominated assets.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and overvalued tech
defensive_moves.rationale: Affordability crunch and subscription fatigue will hit consumer spending. Tech layoffs and valuation compression pose risks.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy gold and precious metals as inflation hedges
defensive_moves.rationale: Despite recent slump, gold remains a hedge against stagflation and geopolitical risk. Copper demand from AI data centers supports long-term outlook.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Add to energy positions selectively on pullbacks
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil prices remain supported by geopolitical risks and supply constraints. Canadian pipeline proposals add long-term value. Take profits on sharp rallies.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile in the next 1-3 months as the Fed transition to Kevin Warsh unfolds, bond yields continue to rise, and stagflation fears persist. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 recently hit record highs but then sold off sharply, indicating fragility. Expect further downside if inflation data remains hot or geopolitical tensions escalate. Defensive positioning and cash are favored.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the outlook is cautiously optimistic for select sectors. AI and data center investment will drive growth for semiconductors, infrastructure, and energy. The IPO market is healthy, indicating private sector confidence. However, stagflation risks, EM debt crises, and potential Fed rate hikes could cap upside. A barbell strategy of AI/growth stocks and defensive/income assets is recommended.
key_catalysts: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting and policy signals
Trump-Xi trade deal progress or lack thereof
Cerebras and other AI IPOs' aftermarket performance
Q2 earnings season and forward guidance
Inflation data (CPI, PPI) and employment reports
Oil price trajectory and OPEC+ decisions
US rare earth policy and supply chain initiatives
Consumer spending data ahead of summer
watch_list: Dollar index (DXY) and EM currency pairs
WTI crude oil price and Brent spread
SpaceX IPO filing details and valuation
Meta layoffs and tech sector hiring data
Mortgage rates and housing market data
